symptoms.symptom_name: Cyclical hyperventilation alternating with prolonged breath-holding (central pattern)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Alternating hyperventilation/apnoea is a hallmark of Rett autonomic breathing dysfunction.
Asthma is expiratory obstruction, not a cyclical central hold–hyperventilate pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Perioral cyanosis with near-syncope during breath-holds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible central cyanosis with near-syncope indicates meaningful desaturation, a driver of escalation.
Recurrent hypoxia is a substrate for a secondary seizure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute trigger — routine disruption plus bright/loud sensory overload

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Distress and sensory overload precipitate autonomic hyper-arousal cycles in Rett syndrome.
Stereotypic hand movements are pathognomonic of Rett syndrome and confirm the underlying condition context.
Not diagnostic, but mandates a lower threshold for in-person review because deterioration cannot be self-reported.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of wheeze / no inspiratory stridor (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No wheeze makes lower-airway obstruction unlikely.
No inspiratory stridor makes a fixed upper-airway obstruction unlikely.
Hypoxia-driven vagal bradycardia in a condition with prolonged-QT/sudden-death risk — sealed clinician measurement, drives ECG-capable assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured SpO2 fluctuating 85–98% and RR 40 during hyperventilation phase

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective desaturation to 85% — sealed clinician-measured value; the AI infers hypoxia from visible cyanosis and near-syncope, not from a measured number.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear lung fields, no murmurs on examination (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear chest on auscultation argues against a primary lower-airway cause — sealed clinician finding.
Only an ECG can exclude a dangerous QT prolongation/arrhythmia — never available via telehealth chat, a driver for in-person emergency assessment.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Rett autonomic breathing crisis with hypoxia
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute Rett-associated central breathing dysregulation crisis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of the cyclical central pattern, an acute stress trigger, hand stereotypies and hypoxic cyanosis identifies the mechanism while the hypoxia mandates escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continuous pulse oximetry and cardiorespiratory monitoring (in an emergency setting)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the depth and frequency of desaturation and detects ongoing hypoxia — requires equipment the family does not have.
Rett syndrome carries prolonged-QT/sudden-death risk; the transient bradycardia makes ECG assessment necessary to exclude an arrhythmia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person paediatric neurological assessment for possible seizure activity
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Recurrent hypoxia can provoke a secondary seizure that cannot be confirmed or excluded remotely.
Confirms clear lung fields and excludes a lower-airway contribution — a clinician examination, not available via chat.
The pattern is central, not bronchospastic; a reliever inhaler is not indicated and would not address a centrally-driven breath-holding cycle.
